title: Hymn IX.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Come rest, and be still in the bosom of Thy Mother, Son of the Glorious.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Forwardness fits not the sons of kings.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: O Son of David, Thou art glorious, and [yet] the Son of Mary, who dost hide Thy beauty in the inner chamber.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: To whom art Thou like, glad Babe, fair little One, Whose Mother is a Virgin, Whose Father is hidden, Whom even the Seraphim are not able to look upon?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Tell us whom Thou art like, O Son of the Gracious!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: When the wrathful came to see Thee, Thou madest them gladsome: they exchanged smiles one with another: the angry were made gentle in Thee, O sweet One.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Blessed art Thou, little One, for that in Thee even the bitter are made sweet.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Who ever saw a Babe that was gladsome when in arms to those that came near him, lo! reached Himself unto them that were far off?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Fair sight [to see] a Child, that takes thought for every man that they may see him!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He that hath care came and saw Thee, and his care fled away.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He that had anxiety; at Thee forgat his anxiety; the hungry by Thee forgat his victuals; and he that had an errand, by Thee was errant and forgot his journey!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: O still Thyself, and let men go to their works!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thou art a son of the poor, learn from Thyself that all the poor had to leave their work to come.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thou who lovest men, hast bound men together by Thy gladsomeness.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: David, that stately king, took branches, and in the feast amongst the children as he danced, he gave praise.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Is it not the love of David Thy father that is warm in Thee?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: That daughter of Saul! her father’s devil spake in her: she called the stately [king] a vile fellow, because he gave an ensample to the elders of her people of taking up branches with the children in the day of praise to Thee.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Who would not fear to lay it to Thee that Thou art forward?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For lo, the daughter of Saul who mocked the child, cut off her womb from childbearing; because her mouth derided, the reward of its mouth was barrenness.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let mouths tremble at blasphemy, lest they be shut up!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Refrain, O daughter of Sion, thy mouth from Him, for He is the Son of David, Who is gladsome before thee.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Be not unto Him as the daughter of Saul, whose race is extinct.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Because Elijah restrained the desire of the body, he withheld rain from the adulterous; because he kept under his body, he withheld dew from the whoremongers, who let their fountains be loosely poured out.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Because the hidden fire of the lust of the body ruled not in him, to him the fire from on high was obedient.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: And since he subdued on the earth the lust of the flesh, he went up thither where holiness dwells and is at peace.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Elisha also who deadened his own body, quickened the dead.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The resurrection of the dead was in the usual course by a sanctification not in the usual course; He raised the child, because he purified his soul like a weaned child.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Moses, who divided and separated himself from his wife, divided the sea before the harlot.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Zipporah though daughter of a heathen priest kept sanctity: with a calf the daughter of Abraham went a whoring.
